provenance.source_corpus: Distribution and potential effects of water beetles in lakes recovering from acidification

source_spans.text: Our data suggest that G. liberus can tolerate a wide range of pH but is eliminated when ﬁsh are present
Mesocosm experiments in Swan Lake, a ﬁshless lake recovering from acidiﬁcation, revealed that larval G. liberus can reduce total zooplankton abundance, species richness, and species diversity.
Mesocosm experiments in Swan Lake, a ﬁshless lake recovering from acidiﬁcation, revealed that larval G. liberus can reduce total zooplankton abundance, species richness, and species diversity.
Mesocosm experiments in Swan Lake, a ﬁshless lake recovering from acidiﬁcation, revealed that larval G. liberus can reduce total zooplankton abundance, species richness, and species diversity.
Our data suggest that G. liberus can tolerate a wide range of pH but is eliminated when ﬁsh are present
However, the occurrence of ﬁsh was signiﬁcantly negatively correlated with water beetle distribution, particularly for the predaceous diving beetle, Graphoderus liberus .
